title: Iran's IRGC threatens 'long and painful strikes' on US positions if attacks resume
summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned that any new US attack, even if limited, would trigger 'long and painful strikes' on US positions in the region, including warships, as the ceasefire deadline approaches.
contents:
## IRGC Threatens Retaliation Against US Forces

### Military Warning
A senior official of Iran's Revolutionary Guards stated that any new US attack on Iran, even if limited, would usher in "long and painful strikes" on US regional positions. Aerospace Force Commander Majid Mousavi amplified the threat, stating: "We've seen what happened to your regional bases, we will see the same thing happen to your warships."

### Context of Damaged US Installations
CNN investigation revealed that Iran and its allies have damaged at least 16 US military installations across eight Middle Eastern countries, rendering some positions virtually unusable. The damaged facilities constitute the majority of US military positions in the region, with targets including advanced radar systems, communications systems, and aircraft.

### Escalation Indicators
- UAE imposed travel bans for citizens to Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq, urging those present to leave immediately
- Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz warned that Israel may "soon have to act again" against Iran
- Trump received briefing on updated military options from Pentagon officials
- US Central Command reportedly prepared a plan for a "short and powerful" wave of strikes

### Force Posture
The US Navy currently maintains 19 ships in the Middle East, including two aircraft carriers, and seven ships in the Indian Ocean. US forces have redirected at least 44 commercial vessels as part of the blockade of Iran and boarded at least three ships.

### Assessment
The threat level remains elevated as the ceasefire appears increasingly fragile, with both sides maintaining aggressive postures while diplomatic channels produce no breakthrough.
